ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and develops naval vessels, including ships, submarines, and offshore platforms, ensuring safety, efficiency, and environmental sustainability.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Naval architects work on the design, development, and testing of naval vessels, considering factors like hydrodynamics, structural integrity, and regulatory compliance. They use computer-aided design (CAD) software, computational fluid dynamics (CFD), and other tools to create detailed designs and models.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.Tech in Naval Architecture or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in naval architecture can pursue a degree in a related field, such as engineering or architecture.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a postgraduate diploma or certification in naval architecture to bridge the gap.

future_builder.description: Experienced naval architects can advance their careers by acquiring specialized skills, such as computational fluid dynamics (CFD) or finite element analysis (FEA).
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: Minimum 5 years of experience as a junior naval architect

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into naval architecture by acquiring relevant skills and experience.
